reasoning: Plan follows the established GeometrySystem interface pattern exactly (init/draw/cleanup from src/visual/types.ts)
New file src/visual/systems/pointCloud.ts is a peer of particleField.ts and waveField.ts — no structural deviation
Registration in ModeManager via main.ts is a minimal addition (third entry in existing modes array)
PRNG usage with seed suffix ':pointcloud' follows the deterministic seeding convention
Buffer management pattern (Float32Array + needsUpdate) matches particleField exactly
Camera drift via mesh rotation is self-contained within the GeometrySystem boundary — no changes to renderLoop or camera setup
Quality tier scaling (low=200, medium=500, high=1200) adds a new maxPoints field to QualityProfile but doesn't alter existing maxParticles values
Test suite mirrors particleField.test.ts patterns with 20 focused tests covering all acceptance criteria

concerns.issue: maxPoints values (200/500/1200) are significantly higher than maxParticles (150/350/600) — the high tier doubles the point budget
concerns.suggestion: Acceptable since Points with sizeAttenuation and no per-particle physics are cheaper than the particleField's per-particle velocity simulation, but verify GPU budget on low-end devices

concerns.issue: Plan adds enableSparkle to PointCloudConfig but this already exists on QualityProfile — the config just passes it through
concerns.suggestion: This is fine as a config forwarding pattern consistent with ParticleFieldConfig, no action needed

concerns.issue: AdditiveBlending on the material may cause visual artifacts when point cloud mode overlaps with mode transitions in ModeManager
concerns.suggestion: ModeManager calls cleanup before init of new mode, so overlap shouldn't occur — verify transition behavior

interface_changes.change: Add maxPoints: number to QualityProfile interface
interface_changes.impact: Additive — no existing consumers break since the field is new. All three tier returns must be updated.

notes: Clean additive change. The plan correctly identifies all files to modify, follows established patterns, and the test coverage is thorough. The Z-depth range (±2.5 vs particleField's ±1) and spherical generation approach are sound design choices for the 'visible depth' acceptance criterion.
